description: Initialise project with Bun, TypeScript strict mode, and test infrastructure
steps: Run `bun init` in project root (if not already done)
Configure tsconfig.json with strict: true, ESM, paths
Create src/ and test/ directory structure per CLAUDE.md
Create a trivial test (test/unit/smoke.test.ts) that passes
Verify `bun test` runs and passes
Add smol-toml dependency (`bun add smol-toml`)
Add msgpackr dependency (`bun add msgpackr`)
Verify both imports work in a test file
verification: `bun test` passes with at least 1 test. `bun build --compile` produces a binary that runs.

description: Implement Metric data model with types, hashId, and copy
steps: Create src/core/metric.ts with Metric class
Implement FieldValue, MetricType, MetricPriority types
Implement tags as sorted Map (sort on construction/mutation)
Implement hashId() using FNV-64a of name + sorted tags
Implement copy() as hand-rolled deep copy
Implement accept(), reject(), drop() tracking methods
Create factory function createMetric()
Write unit tests in test/unit/core/metric.test.ts
verification: All tests pass: hashId determinism, copy independence, tag sorting, field type round-trips, factory defaults.
tests: Metric created with all 4 field types (number, bigint, string, boolean)
hashId() same for same name+tags across calls
hashId() same regardless of tag insertion order
hashId() differs when name changes
hashId() differs when any tag key or value changes
copy() produces independent instance (mutate copy, original unchanged)
copy() preserves name, tags, fields, timestamp, type, priority
createMetric() defaults: timestamp set, type=untyped, priority=normal
Tags are sorted by key in iteration order

description: Implement Channel<T> with ring buffer and drop-oldest overflow
steps: Create src/core/channel.ts with Channel<T> class
Implement ring buffer with configurable capacity
Implement send() with drop-oldest when full
Implement receive() as AsyncGenerator<T>
Implement close(), length, capacity, closed properties
Write unit tests in test/unit/core/channel.test.ts
verification: All tests pass: basic send/receive, capacity overflow with drop-oldest, close semantics, async generator completion.
tests: Send N items, receive N items in correct order (FIFO)
Send capacity+5 items, receive only last `capacity` items (oldest dropped)
close() → send() returns false
close() → receive() drains remaining items then completes (done=true)
receive() on empty open channel blocks until item sent
length property tracks current item count
capacity property returns configured capacity
closed property is false initially, true after close()
Concurrent producer/consumer: producer sends 1000, consumer receives 1000

description: Implement Broadcaster<T> with independent per-consumer channels
steps: Add Broadcaster<T> class to src/core/channel.ts
Implement addConsumer(), removeConsumer(), broadcast(), closeAll()
broadcast() takes a copy function and sends deep copies to each consumer
Each consumer channel handles overflow independently
Write unit tests in test/unit/core/broadcaster.test.ts
verification: All tests pass: multi-consumer broadcast, independent overflow, copy isolation, close propagation.
tests: 3 consumers each receive all broadcast items
Broadcast uses copy function (consumers get independent copies, not references)
Consumer A's channel full and dropping → Consumer B and C unaffected
removeConsumer() stops delivery to that consumer
closeAll() closes all consumer channels
Broadcast to zero consumers is a no-op (no error)

steps: Write test/integration/channel-metric.test.ts
Create Channel<Metric>, send real Metric objects, receive and verify data integrity
Create Broadcaster<Metric>, broadcast to 2 channels, verify independent copies via metric.copy()
Verify hashId() is consistent after channel transit
verification: Metrics flow through channels with full data integrity. Broadcaster copies are independent.
tests: 100 metrics through Channel<Metric> — all fields/tags/timestamps preserved
Broadcaster copies to 2 consumers — mutating one consumer's metric doesn't affect the other's
hashId() is identical before send and after receive

description: Implement Ticker with dual-clock design and clock jump detection
steps: Create src/core/ticker.ts with Ticker class
Implement tick() as AsyncGenerator<number> with interval parameter
Implement dual-clock: Bun.nanoseconds() for elapsed, Date.now() for alignment
Implement aligned mode (fire at interval boundaries)
Implement clock jump detection (>2x interval → re-anchor + warn)
Implement jitter (random per-tick delay)
Implement offset (fixed delay from boundary)
Write unit tests in test/unit/core/ticker.test.ts
verification: All tests pass: interval accuracy, alignment, sequence numbers, cancellation, jitter range, no drift accumulation.
tests: 100ms ticker fires ~10 times in 1100ms (tolerance: 8-12 ticks)
Sequence numbers increment from 0
Ticker can be cancelled via break in for-await-of
Jitter: with 50ms jitter on 200ms interval, ticks are within [200, 250] spacing
50 ticks at 50ms interval: total elapsed is 2500ms ±500ms (no drift accumulation)
alignToInterval helper: 10000ms interval, returns next boundary from epoch

description: Implement Accumulator with auto-timestamp and global tags
steps: Create src/core/accumulator.ts with Accumulator class
Constructor takes Channel<Metric> and optional global tags
Implement addFields() — creates Metric, sends to channel
Implement addMetric() — sends existing metric to channel
Implement addError() — logs and counts, never throws
Auto-timestamp: BigInt(Date.now()) * 1_000_000n when not provided
Global tag merging: global tags + per-metric tags, per-metric wins on conflict
Write unit tests in test/unit/core/accumulator.test.ts
verification: All tests pass: metric creation, auto-timestamp, global tag merge, error counting.
tests: addFields() creates metric with correct name, fields, tags in channel
addFields() auto-assigns nanosecond timestamp when not provided
addFields() uses explicit timestamp when provided (does not overwrite)
addFields() merges global tags with local tags
addFields() local tag wins on conflict with global tag
addMetric() sends metric to channel unmodified
addError() increments error count
addError() does not throw
Multiple addFields() calls produce multiple metrics in channel

steps: Write test/integration/accumulator-channel.test.ts
Accumulator writes metrics via addFields, consumer reads from channel
Verify full data flow including global tags and auto-timestamps
verification: Metrics created by Accumulator flow through Channel and arrive at consumer correctly.
tests: Accumulator addFields → Channel → consumer receives correct metric
Global tags present on received metric
Auto-timestamp is reasonable (within last few seconds)

description: Define plugin interfaces and implement plugin registry
steps: Create src/core/plugin-types.ts with all interfaces
Define: Input, ServiceInput, Processor, Aggregator, Output, StatefulPlugin
Create src/core/plugin-registry.ts
Implement registerPlugin(), getPlugin(), listPlugins()
Plugin metadata: name, type, description
Factory function to create instances
Write unit tests in test/unit/core/plugin-registry.test.ts
verification: All tests pass: registration, lookup, listing, factory instantiation.
tests: Register a mock input plugin, retrieve by name
Register multiple plugins, listPlugins returns all
getPlugin() returns undefined for unregistered name
Factory creates new instance each call (not singleton)
Register same name twice → error or overwrite (decide and document)

description: Implement config parser with TOML, env vars, and duration parsing
steps: Create src/core/config.ts
TOML parsing via smol-toml
Implement env var expansion: ${VAR}, ${VAR:-default}, ${VAR:?error}
Implement duration string parsing: '10s' → 10000, '5m' → 300000, etc.
Define Zod schema for global agent config
Plugin alias uniqueness validation
Secret reference detection (@{store:key}) — mark but don't resolve
Clear error messages for all failure modes
Write unit tests in test/unit/core/config.test.ts
verification: All tests pass: TOML parsing, env var expansion, duration parsing, validation errors, alias uniqueness.
tests: Parse valid TOML config → correct structure
${HOME} resolves to actual env var value
${MISSING:-fallback} resolves to 'fallback'
${MISSING:?must set this} throws with 'must set this' in message
Invalid duration string → clear error
Invalid TOML → error with line info
Duplicate plugin aliases → error naming both aliases
@{secrets:key} detected and preserved (not resolved)
Missing config file → helpful error message
Plugin sections extracted correctly (inputs.modbus, processors.rename, etc.)

description: Implement pipeline runtime with startup, shutdown, and data flow
steps: Create src/pipeline/runtime.ts
Implement pipeline assembly from config: channels, plugins, wiring
Implement startup sequence (simplified: no SQLite, no Hub, no Web UI)
Implement gather loop: Ticker → Input.gather(acc) with timeout
Implement processor chain: sequential processing
Implement aggregator fork: copy to aggregator + auto-forward originals
Implement output flush loop: batch from channel → Output.write()
Implement graceful shutdown: SIGINT/SIGTERM → drain → close
Write unit tests with mock plugins in test/unit/pipeline/runtime.test.ts
verification: All tests pass: end-to-end flow, processor chaining, aggregator fork, gather timeout, graceful shutdown.
tests: Mock input → mock output: metrics flow correctly
2 processors in chain: output sees both transformations applied
Aggregator: originals pass through AND aggregator receives copies
drop_original=true: originals do NOT reach output
Gather timeout: slow input (5s) with 1s timeout → error logged, no crash
Graceful shutdown: SIGTERM → all close() methods called, pipeline drains
2 mock inputs fan-in to 1 channel → output sees metrics from both
Broadcaster: 2 mock outputs each receive all metrics independently

description: Full pipeline integration test: config → runtime → data flow → shutdown
steps: Write test/integration/pipeline-e2e.test.ts
Create a TOML config with a mock input, rename processor, and mock output
Parse config, build pipeline, start it, let it run for a few ticks
Verify metrics flow from input through processor to output with correct transforms
Send SIGTERM equivalent, verify graceful shutdown
Verify all metrics that entered the pipeline reached the output
verification: Full pipeline works end-to-end from config file to data delivery to graceful shutdown.
tests: Config-driven pipeline: TOML → parse → build → start → verify output → shutdown
Rename processor actually renames fields in output metrics
Metric count: input produced N metrics, output received N metrics (no loss)
Shutdown: all plugins get close() called, exit code 0
